attacking_style: Pure sprinter who times final 200m acceleration through traffic. Not an attacker—relies on positioning and power in bunch sprints, showing improved craft in navigating congested finales.
team_tactics: Dependent on full leadout train; struggled when domestiques depleted or absent. Bora/Bahrain provided strong positioning but conversion rate inconsistent. Less effective when forced to navigate alone.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints on straightforward finales. Exposed on repeated circuit climbs and any terrain requiring climbing durability—dropped by classics specialists like Sagan/Colbrelli on undulating courses.
vulnerability: Cannot match top-tier sprinters on pure power when leadout is perfect. Lacks climbing durability for hilly sprint stages. Mental fragility in conversion—multiple second/third places without wins until 2023 breakthrough.
key_patterns: Consistently reaches top-3 positioning but struggles to convert to wins (2019, 2023 Tour).
Dropped from sprint groups on repeated climbs/circuits, lacking durability (2021, exposed twice).
Improved tactical maturity: road-switching when boxed, mental recovery from crashes (2022, 2023).
Team leadout dependency critical; performance drops when isolated or domestiques depleted (2022 Catalunya, 2019).
Performance ceiling below top-tier sprinters despite team support (2022 Eschborn, 2019 BinckBank).
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 shows breakthrough mental resilience and conversion ability after years of near-misses, though Tour top-3s suggest ceiling remains
